properties.name: text
properties.description: The text content to display. While simple Markdown formatting is supported (i.e. without HTML, images, or links), utilizing dedicated UI components is generally preferred for a richer and more structured presentation.

properties.name: variant
properties.description: A hint for the base text style.

properties.name: url
properties.description: The URL of the image to display.

properties.name: fit
properties.description: Specifies how the image should be resized to fit its container. This corresponds to the CSS 'object-fit' property.

properties.name: variant
properties.description: A hint for the image size and style.

properties.name: name
properties.description: The name of the icon to display.

properties.name: url
properties.description: The URL of the video to display.

properties.name: url
properties.description: The URL of the audio to be played.

properties.name: description
properties.description: A description of the audio, such as a title or summary.

components.name: Row
components.description: A layout component that arranges its children horizontally. To create a grid layout, nest Columns within this Row.

properties.name: children
properties.description: Defines the children. Use an array of strings for a fixed set of children, or a template object to generate children from a data list. Children cannot be defined inline, they must be referred to by ID.

properties.name: justify
properties.description: Defines the arrangement of children along the main axis (horizontally). Use 'spaceBetween' to push items to the edges, or 'start'/'end'/'center' to pack them together.

properties.name: align
properties.description: Defines the alignment of children along the cross axis (vertically). This is similar to the CSS 'align-items' property, but uses camelCase values (e.g., 'start').

components.name: Column
components.description: A layout component that arranges its children vertically. To create a grid layout, nest Rows within this Column.

properties.name: children
properties.description: Defines the children. Use an array of strings for a fixed set of children, or a template object to generate children from a data list. Children cannot be defined inline, they must be referred to by ID.

properties.name: justify
properties.description: Defines the arrangement of children along the main axis (vertically). Use 'spaceBetween' to push items to the edges (e.g. header at top, footer at bottom), or 'start'/'end'/'center' to pack them together.

properties.name: align
properties.description: Defines the alignment of children along the cross axis (horizontally). This is similar to the CSS 'align-items' property.

properties.name: children
properties.description: Defines the children. Use an array of strings for a fixed set of children, or a template object to generate children from a data list.

properties.name: direction
properties.description: The direction in which the list items are laid out.

properties.name: align
properties.description: Defines the alignment of children along the cross axis.

properties.name: child
properties.description: The ID of the single child component to be rendered inside the card. To display multiple elements, you MUST wrap them in a layout component (like Column or Row) and pass that container's ID here. Do NOT pass multiple IDs or a non-existent ID. Do NOT define the child component inline.

properties.name: tabs
properties.description: An array of objects, where each object defines a tab with a title and a child component.

properties.name: trigger
properties.description: The ID of the component that opens the modal when interacted with (e.g., a button). Do NOT define the component inline.

properties.name: content
properties.description: The ID of the component to be displayed inside the modal. Do NOT define the component inline.

properties.name: axis
properties.description: The orientation of the divider.

properties.name: child
properties.description: The ID of the child component. Use a 'Text' component for a labeled button. Only use an 'Icon' if the requirements explicitly ask for an icon-only button. Do NOT define the child component inline.

properties.name: variant
properties.description: A hint for the button style. If omitted, a default button style is used. 'primary' indicates this is the main call-to-action button. 'borderless' means the button has no visual border or background, making its child content appear like a clickable link.

properties.name: label
properties.description: The text label for the input field.

properties.name: value
properties.description: The value of the text field.

properties.name: variant
properties.description: The type of input field to display.

properties.name: label
properties.description: The text to display next to the checkbox.

properties.name: value
properties.description: The current state of the checkbox (true for checked, false for unchecked).

components.name: ChoicePicker
components.description: A component that allows selecting one or more options from a list.

properties.name: label
properties.description: The label for the group of options.

properties.name: variant
properties.description: A hint for how the choice picker should be displayed and behave.

properties.name: options
properties.description: The list of available options to choose from.

properties.name: value
properties.description: The list of currently selected values. This should be bound to a string array in the data model.

properties.name: displayStyle
properties.description: The display style of the component.

properties.name: filterable
properties.description: If true, displays a search input to filter the options.

properties.name: label
properties.description: The label for the slider.

properties.name: min
properties.description: The minimum value of the slider.

properties.name: max
properties.description: The maximum value of the slider.

properties.name: value
properties.description: The current value of the slider.

properties.name: value
properties.description: The selected date and/or time value in ISO 8601 format. If not yet set, initialize with an empty string.

properties.name: enableDate
properties.description: If true, allows the user to select a date.

properties.name: enableTime
properties.description: If true, allows the user to select a time.

properties.name: min
properties.description: The minimum allowed date/time in ISO 8601 format.

properties.name: max
properties.description: The maximum allowed date/time in ISO 8601 format.

properties.name: label
properties.description: The text label for the input field.

functions.name: required
functions.description: Checks that the value is not null, undefined, or empty.

args.name: value
args.description: The value to check.

functions.name: regex
functions.description: Checks that the value matches a regular expression string.

args.name: pattern
args.description: The regex pattern to match against.

args.name: min
args.description: The minimum allowed length.

args.name: max
args.description: The maximum allowed length.

args.name: min
args.description: The minimum allowed value.

args.name: max
args.description: The maximum allowed value.

functions.name: email
functions.description: Checks that the value is a valid email address.

functions.name: formatString
functions.description: Performs string interpolation of data model values and other functions in the catalog functions list and returns the resulting string. The value string can contain interpolated expressions in the `${expression}` format. Supported expression types include: JSON Pointer paths to the data model (e.g., `${/absolute/path}` or `${relative/path}`), and client-side function calls (e.g., `${now()}`). Function arguments must be named (e.g., `${formatDate(value:${/currentDate}, format:'MM-dd')}`). To include a literal `${` sequence, escape it as `\${`.

functions.name: formatNumber
functions.description: Formats a number with the specified grouping and decimal precision.

args.name: value
args.description: The number to format.

args.name: decimals
args.description: Optional. The number of decimal places to show. Defaults to 0 or 2 depending on locale.

args.name: grouping
args.description: Optional. If true, uses locale-specific grouping separators (e.g. '1,000'). If false, returns raw digits (e.g. '1000'). Defaults to true.

functions.name: formatCurrency
functions.description: Formats a number as a currency string.

args.name: value
args.description: The monetary amount.

args.name: currency
args.description: The ISO 4217 currency code (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR').

args.name: decimals
args.description: Optional. The number of decimal places to show. Defaults to 0 or 2 depending on locale.

args.name: grouping
args.description: Optional. If true, uses locale-specific grouping separators (e.g. '1,000'). If false, returns raw digits (e.g. '1000'). Defaults to true.

functions.name: formatDate
functions.description: Formats a timestamp into a string using a pattern.

args.name: value
args.description: The date to format.

args.name: format
args.description:
A Unicode TR35 date pattern string.

Token Reference:
- Year: 'yy' (26), 'yyyy' (2026)
- Month: 'M' (1), 'MM' (01), 'MMM' (Jan), 'MMMM' (January)
- Day: 'd' (1), 'dd' (01), 'E' (Tue), 'EEEE' (Tuesday)
- Hour (12h): 'h' (1-12), 'hh' (01-12) - requires 'a' for AM/PM
- Hour (24h): 'H' (0-23), 'HH' (00-23) - Military Time
- Minute: 'mm' (00-59)
- Second: 'ss' (00-59)
- Period: 'a' (AM/PM)

Examples:
- 'MMM dd, yyyy' -> 'Jan 16, 2026'
- 'HH:mm' -> '14:30' (Military)
- 'h:mm a' -> '2:30 PM'
- 'EEEE, d MMMM' -> 'Friday, 16 January'

functions.name: pluralize
functions.description: Returns a localized string based on the Common Locale Data Repository (CLDR) plural category of the count (zero, one, two, few, many, other). Requires an 'other' fallback. For English, just use 'one' and 'other'.

args.name: value
args.description: The numeric value used to determine the plural category.

args.name: zero
args.description: String for the 'zero' category (e.g., 0 items).

args.name: one
args.description: String for the 'one' category (e.g., 1 item).

args.name: two
args.description: String for the 'two' category (used in Arabic, Welsh, etc.).

args.name: few
args.description: String for the 'few' category (e.g., small groups in Slavic languages).

args.name: many
args.description: String for the 'many' category (e.g., large groups in various languages).

args.name: other
args.description: The default/fallback string (used for general plural cases).

functions.name: openUrl
functions.description: Opens the specified URL in a browser or handler. This function has no return value.

args.name: url
args.description: The URL to open.

functions.name: and
functions.description: Performs a logical AND operation on a list of boolean values.

args.name: values
args.description: The list of boolean values to evaluate.

functions.name: or
functions.description: Performs a logical OR operation on a list of boolean values.

args.name: values
args.description: The list of boolean values to evaluate.

functions.name: not
functions.description: Performs a logical NOT operation on a boolean value.

args.name: value
args.description: The boolean value to negate.

theme_properties.name: primaryColor
theme_properties.description: The primary brand color used for highlights (e.g., primary buttons, active borders). Renderers may generate variants of this color for different contexts. Format: Hexadecimal code (e.g., '#00BFFF').

theme_properties.name: iconUrl
theme_properties.description: A URL for an image that identifies the agent or tool associated with the surface.

theme_properties.name: agentDisplayName
theme_properties.description: Text to be displayed next to the surface to identify the agent or tool that created it.
